title: Content cutover + referential closure. Points the stack's Directus consumers at their OWN per-stack Directus (re-point cms's DIRECTUS_BASE_ADDR to the in-network http://directus:8055; keep DIRECTUS_PUBLIC_BASE_ADDR / the asset plane on prod so images stay real) and guarantees the served catalog is REFERENTIALLY CLOSED — a measured cross-surface closure gene (no content row references a taxonomy node-id the captured public taxonomy lacks). Wires the directus_files ref capture (Fate-3 from M21/DEF-M21-03, as a referenced-subset) + studio-desk's local instance + a locally-minted static admin token. The data-plane goes local; the asset-plane stays prod.
position_note: M23 is the 3rd milestone of v1.5 'prop room' (M21->M25). M21 made the directus replay exit 0 + serve anonymously; M22 boots + verifies the per-stack instance as a compose service; M23 points services at it (re-point DIRECTUS_BASE_ADDR) + closes referential gaps; M24 tells the corpus-wide truth + the hygiene strand; M25 proves it live. Strictly sequential. After this close: M24 (/developer-kit:build-milestone, section shape — docs convergence + hygiene).
kind: a section milestone — production code in the SEPARATE gitignored rosetta-extensions authoring copy. M23 is a Go-AND-python milestone (unlike M22): Go stack-snapshot (directus/{directus,media,provision}.go, firewall/firewall.go, manifest/manifest.go, replay/replay.go, capture/capture.go, cmd/{stacksnap,provision-plan}) + Go stack-seeding/dna (snapshot.go, fidelity_probe.go) + python stack-core/gen_override.py + stack-injection/gen_injected_override.py + dev-stack/dev-setdress.sh. The rosetta m23/content-cutover branch carries ONLY plan/doc records + the 6 corpus docs (cms/studio-desk/jobsimulation/next-web-app env truth + safety.md retire-live-read + snapshot-spec.md the closure gene + directus_files-wired). ALL executable code is ext-repo-side (tag prop-room-m23, set by the orchestrator post-close).

tests.language: Go (stack-snapshot + stack-seeding, the M23-touched modules) + python (stack-core + stack-injection override generators, stdlib unittest). The rosetta-side milestone branch carries no executable code; its deliverables are the 6 corpus docs.
tests.go_delta_note: M23 added Go tests to the two modules it touched: stack-snapshot 290->316 (+26: directus_files referenced-subset + ClearByDelete + the firewall admit-iff + provision ValidateProvisionable) · stack-seeding 252->259 (+7: the cross-surface closure probe / gene). The untouched modules (alignment, clerkenstein) are unchanged by M23. Method: Test+Fuzz func-decl count via `grep -rohE '^func (Test|Fuzz)'`; the +49 total absorbs a small baseline re-count of the untouched modules between the M22 method and this consistent count (alignment 43->46, clerkenstein 210->215) — those modules were NOT touched by M23; their delta is a counting-method reconciliation, not new tests. M23's OWN contribution is +33 Go test funcs across stack-snapshot + stack-seeding.
tests.python_delta_note: M23 touched 2 python suites: stack-core 61->69 (+8, the §1 dev env-emission / DIRECTUS_BASE_ADDR re-point tests) · stack-injection (110 ran, 8 env-gated skips -> 102 active; +the §2/§3 demo cutover + studio-desk minted-token tests). The other 3 python suites (dev-stack, demo-stack, stack-verify) are untouched by M23. Counts via python3 -m unittest discover (the suites are stdlib unittest, not pytest).
tests.test_count_method: Go: go test -count=1 ./... per module (fresh, not cached); func count via grep '^func (Test|Fuzz)'. Python: python3 -m unittest discover -s tests -p 'test_*.py' per touched module dir. 0 failures across all 4 suites. stack-injection's 8 skips are env-gated (require docker/compose absent in the authoring harness) — expected, not failures.
tests.lint: go vet CLEAN on both touched Go modules; shellcheck CLEAN on dev-stack/dev-setdress.sh; py_compile CLEAN on both touched .py generators.
tests.flake_gate: 5/5 clean on all 4 touched suites — Go stack-snapshot + stack-seeding run with -count=1 -shuffle=on (5/5 ok each); python stack-core + stack-injection run sequentially 5x (5/5 ok each). 0 failures across all runs. Note: pre-existing ResourceWarnings (unclosed files) appear in test lines M23 did NOT touch (test_stack_registry.py, pre-M23 test_injection.py open().read() idioms) — a known pre-existing hygiene smell, warnings not failures, carried not fixed.

coverage.note: M23 ran a 2-pass hardening sweep (recorded inline in progress.md § Hardening, no separate harden-milestone ledger). Pass 1 closed the highest-priority gap: stack-seeding/dna PgFidelityProbe.CrossSurfaceDangling 0% -> 100% (the new gene's probe) + cmd/stacksnap CountTenantRows 93.1% -> 100% + firewall/manifest/replay behavioral pins. Pass 2 closed directus ValidateProvisionable 80% -> 100% (the prod-safety-before-token-gate propagation). The python generators were already fully covered (gen_override.py 87%, gen_injected_override.py 99%). The remaining unitable surface is the DB-only concrete-conn executors (ClearForReplay/CopyIn/provision DDL) — structurally unit-untestable; their pure SQL-builder seams ARE 100%.
coverage.harden_bugs_note: 0 production bugs surfaced across the 2 hardening passes + the close review — every gap was a test gap, not a code bug. The close adversarial pass (4 scenarios: filter slipping the admit-iff gate, a valid token masking a prod BaseAddr, a two-dest scan on empty/error, the directus_settings FK on TRUNCATE) found 0 new findings — all already test-pinned. See decisions.md § Adversarial review.

benchmarks.note: No performance-sensitive code in M23 (env-emission glue, a firewall admit branch, a cross-surface CTE measured non-blockingly outside the bring-up path). The cross-surface closure gene is criticality 'standard' (surfaces in the overall score, does not block the CRITICAL gate; measure-snapshot is not run at bring-up so it never blocks UP).

completeness_ledger_section.known_issue_prod_data: K-AIFUNX-E658 — 2 public published sims reference a node existing only as a customer-scoped skill. Uncloseable by tooling (capturing it = firewall breach; editing prod = forbidden). The cross-surface closure gene MEASURES + NAMES it (criticality standard, non-blocking) rather than silently producing an empty picker. Operator-owned prod data correction, outside tooling scope. Recorded M23-D5 + snapshot-spec.md.

notes: M23 is the 3rd + cutover milestone of v1.5 'prop room' — it points the data plane at the local Directus (re-point cms's DIRECTUS_BASE_ADDR to the in-network http://directus:8055; the asset plane stays on prod so images stay real, the user's explicit call) and guarantees referential closure via a MEASURED cross-surface gene. The load-bearing data-design finding: full-taxonomy capture (organization_id IS NULL) was ALREADY the state, so closure is maximal by construction — the only way a content ref can dangle is if it points at a non-public node, which the firewall MUST NOT capture; the gene MEASURES + NAMES that residual (prod has exactly 1: K-AIFUNX-E658) rather than silently shipping an empty picker. M23 RESOLVED 2 inherited M21 deferrals in-milestone (directus_files ref capture + referential closure). Close was clean: 6 findings (0 scope / 0 code-quality / 0 adversarial-new / 1 docs Fate-1 fixed / 0 tests / 5 decision-triage backref tags), the deferral audit GREEN (2 inherited RESOLVED, 0 repeat / 0 aged, the K-AIFUNX-E658 prod residual fated operator-owned KNOWN-ISSUE). Go 795 -> 844 (+49 total; +33 M23-own across stack-snapshot+stack-seeding, the rest a counting-method reconciliation on untouched modules); python touched suites stack-core 61->69 + stack-injection 110; flake 0 (5/5). Next: M24 (/developer-kit:build-milestone — docs convergence + the 4-item hygiene strand).
